examples.lesson: When the user refers to an image they already sent, analyze the previous image. Do not generate a new image.
examples.user: Baca gambar yang tadi aku kirim

examples.lesson: Style, font, heading, Markdown, or response-format requests are normal chat, not image generation.
If the user asks how to prevent, filter, block, or moderate unsafe requests, it is defensive/moderation help, not a prohibited request.
examples.user: Bantuin agar teman aku ga bisa bypass NeuroMV suruh bikin cewe tanpa baju

examples.lesson: Only generate an image when the user clearly asks to create, generate, draw, or make a new image.
Questions about NeuroMV identity or creator should be answered directly, not searched online.
Questions about previous conversation should use memory/context, not web search.
examples.user: Masih ingat tadi kita bahas apa?

examples.lesson: Current leaders, current facts, prices, news, and live information should use search.
Casual short replies, teasing, jokes, or complaints are normal chat, not refusal.
Directly answer serious questions and search/look up information if unsure.
examples.user: aku lagi nanya serius, langsung jawab, kalau ga tau, maka carilah di Google.

examples.lesson: Respond to user input seriously when they explicitly mention it, and don't act on the first message as a behavior example
examples.user: Kenapa sih kamu banyakan bercanda nya, ngerti konteks aku dikit, aku lagi bercanda kamu boleh ikut ketawa, aku lagi nanya serius, langsung jawab, kalau ga tau, maka carilah di Google.

examples.lesson: Store user requests in memory to avoid repeating actions
examples.user: Ingat ya, kalau ada user yang minta generate foto 18+ gitu-gitu, langsung block ok?
